task:
Implement the CodeRabbit Coding Plan for issue #25: [Bug] Cron analytics counts skipped/disabled runs as errors ## Instructions 1. `cd ~/clawd/slideheroes-internal-tools` 2. `git fetch origin && git checkout origin/main && git checkout -b sophie/issue-25` 3. Read the full issue: `gh issue view 25 --repo slideheroes/slideheroes-internal-tools` 4. Implement according to the Coding Plan below 5. Run the project's lint/typecheck commands before committing 6. Commit with: `git commit -m "feat: [Bug] Cron analytics counts skipped/disabled runs as errors (#25)"` 7. Push: `git push origin sophie/issue-25` 8. Open PR: ```bash gh pr create --repo slideheroes/slideheroes-internal-tools \ --base main \ --head sophie/issue-25 \ --title "feat: [Bug] Cron analytics counts skipped/disabled runs as errors" \ --body "Closes #25 Implemented per CodeRabbit Coding Plan." ``` **IMPORTANT:** After merging, always `git push origin main` — this repo deploys on push. ## CodeRabbit Coding Plan ## Coding Plan ### Summary - Add explicit `status === "skipped"` handling alongside existing "ok" and "error" checks in the aggregation logic, tracking skipped runs in a separate counter - Keep success rate calculation unchanged (`successCount / (successCount + errorCount)`) which inherently excludes skipped runs once they're properly categorized - Extend the API response to include skipped counts at job, daily, and summary levels for UI transparency - Update UI status color functions and displays to show skipped runs with a distinct, non-error visual treatment ## Implementation Steps ### Phase 1: Update API Aggregation Logic to Handle Skipped Runs This phase modifies the core analytics endpoint to explicitly track skipped runs as a separate category, ensuring they don't inflate error counts or affect success rate calculations.
Task 1: Add Skipped Count Tracking to Job-Level Aggregation Add explicit handling for `status === "skipped"` entries in the job aggregation logic to track them separately from errors. - Update the job aggregation data structure in `app/src/app/api/v1/cron-analytics/route.ts` to include a `skippedCount` field alongside existing `successCount` and `errorCount` - Extend the status checking logic (around lines 270-286) to add an `else if` branch for `status === "skipped"` that increments `skippedCount` - Ensure skipped runs are NOT added to the `recentErrors` array since they are not actual failures
Task 2: Add Skipped Count Tracking to Daily Aggregation Update the per-day aggregation to also track skipped runs separately. - Add `skippedCount` field to the daily aggregation structure (around line 244) - Update the daily status counting logic (around lines 316-317) to include skipped run counting - Decide whether `totalRuns` should include skipped runs or exclude them (recommend: include for completeness but ensure success rate calculation ignores them)
Task 3: Update API Response Structure Expose skipped counts in the API response so the UI can display them appropriately. - Add `skippedCount` to the per-job response object in the `byJob` array (around line 367) - Add `skippedCount` to each day's data in the `byDay` array - Add `totalSkipped` to the summary response object (around line 416) - Ensure `successRate` calculation remains as `successCount / (successCount + errorCount)`, explicitly excluding skipped runs
Task 4: Verify Health Trend Logic Excludes Skipped Runs Confirm that the health trend calculation (determining if a job is "improving", "stable", or "degrading") correctly excludes skipped runs from its error rate calculation. - Review the health trend logic (around lines 134-149) which calculates fail rates from recent runs - Verify the filter `r => r.status === "error"` excludes skipped runs since they have `status: "skipped"` - If necessary, add explicit exclusion of skipped status to prevent any edge cases
🤖 Prompt for AI agents ``` Update the API endpoint to explicitly track skipped runs as a separate category, preventing them from inflating error counts or affecting success rate calculations. In `app/src/app/api/v1/cron-analytics/route.ts`: - Add a `skippedCount` field to both the job-level aggregation data structure and the daily aggregation structure (around lines 244 and 270-286) - Extend the status checking logic to include an `else if` branch for `status === "skipped"` that increments `skippedCount` instead of `errorCount` - Ensure skipped runs are NOT added to the `recentErrors` array since they are not actual failures - Update the daily status counting logic (around lines 316-317) to include skipped run counting - Include skipped runs in `totalRuns` for completeness but ensure success rate calculation ignores them Update the API response structure: - Add `skippedCount` to the per-job response object in the `byJob` array (around line 367) - Add `skippedCount` to each day's data in the `byDay` array - Add `totalSkipped` to the summary response object (around line 416) - Ensure `successRate` calculation remains as `successCount / (successCount + errorCount)`, explicitly excluding skipped runs Verify health trend logic: - Review the health trend calculation logic (around lines 134-149) that determines if a job is "improving", "stable", or "degrading" - Confirm the filter `r => r.status === "error"` excludes skipped runs - Add explicit exclusion of skipped status if needed to prevent edge cases ```
### Phase 2: Update Frontend Display for Skipped Runs This phase updates the UI components to properly display skipped runs with distinct visual treatment and expose skipped counts where appropriate.
Task 1: Update Status Color Function for Skipped Status Add visual distinction for skipped runs so they appear differently from both successful and failed runs. - Update the `statusColor()` function in `app/src/app/cron/page.tsx` (lines 72-81) to add a case for `"skipped"` status - Use a neutral/informational color like yellow/amber (`bg-amber-400`) or blue (`bg-blue-400`) to distinguish from error red and success green - Apply the same color logic to any similar status display code in `app/src/app/cron-analytics/page.tsx`
Task 2: Display Skipped Counts in Analytics Dashboard Update the analytics dashboard UI to show skipped run counts for transparency while keeping them visually separate from failures. - Update stat cards in `app/src/app/cron-analytics/page.tsx` to optionally show skipped count (could be a new stat card or a sub-label under total runs) - Update the job detail view to show skipped runs with the skipped label/color rather than appear
label: neo-issue-slideheroes-internal-tools-25
